steps.title: Spot the tempting but flawed reasoning
steps.content: The passage frames a tempting but flawed reasoning: “although similarities in the isotopic signatures of elements detected in Mars's atmosphere and in Martian meteorites recovered on Earth make it tempting to treat the geochemical properties of the meteorites as ______ those of Mars's interiors, Mars's geology cannot be ascertained based solely on meteorite-sample analyses.” The blank should express the natural inference the researchers warn against.

steps.title: Find what fits the inference
steps.content:
“Proxies for” means representatives or substitutes—meteorites would serve as stand-ins to understand Mars's interiors, which captures the tempting reasoning.
The other three either misname what meteorites would represent (contrivances = artificial schemes, catalysts = triggers for change, deterrents = obstacles).
That confirms A is correct.
